content.problemStatement:
Given two strings SSS and TTT, find the length of their longest common subsequence (LCS). A subsequence is formed by removing zero or more characters from a string while maintaining the relative order of the remaining characters. The LCS is a subsequence that is present in both strings.

For example, if S="abcde"S = \text{"abcde"}S="abcde" and T="ace"T = \text{"ace"}T="ace", then "ace"\text{"ace"}"ace" is a common subsequence of length 3.


Input Format

First line contains TTT - the number of test cases
For each test case:

Single line containing two space-separated strings SSS and TTT




Output Format

For each test case:

Print a single integer - the length of the longest common subsequence

sampleTestCases.input:
3
abcde ace
hello world
aa aa
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1:

S="abcde"S = \text{"abcde"}S="abcde", T="ace"T = \text{"ace"}T="ace"
LCS is "ace" of length 3

Test Case 2:

S="hello"S = \text{"hello"}S="hello", T="world"T = \text{"world"}T="world"
LCS is length 1

Test Case 3:

S="aa"S = \text{"aa"}S="aa", T="aa"T = \text{"aa"}T="aa"
LCS is "aa" of length 2

hidden.input:
3
abcde ace
hello world
aa aa
